steps.title: Read the second sentence as a definition of the blank
steps.content: The follow-up explains that the early Moon was softer, impacts left less of an impression, and therefore evidence of early impacts may no longer be present. So the surface cannot serve as a record that shows early impact events.

steps.title: Pick the word that means "to show as evidence of"
steps.content: We need a verb meaning to display or be evidence of something. Reflect carries exactly that sense (as in "the data reflect a trend").

steps.title: Eliminate the other choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — reflect: matches "show evidence of" — the soft early surface cannot reflect those impacts. ✓
  • Choice B — receive: the Moon already received the impacts; the issue is preserving/showing them, not accepting them. ✗
  • Choice C — evaluate: a surface does not assess or judge events — wrong sense entirely. ✗
  • Choice D — mimic: overreach — to mimic is to imitate, not to record evidence of. ✗
That confirms A is correct.
